title: How exactly was Eva released in the USA
display_title: How exactly was Eva released in the USA
wikitext:
[]: How exactly was Eva released in the USA?             Quote Post by BritKnee » Fri May 20, 2022 8:06 pm How did eva make it to the USA? Was it first on tape like the OVA's of the day or did ADV broadcast it somewhere? Also what was it like being an Eva fan in the west from 1996 to 1999 or so? Also how much were the original tapes?

[]: Re: How exactly was Eva released in the USA?             Quote Post by Szmitten » Fri May 20, 2022 10:01 pm ADV licensed Evangelon pretty quick and the first two episode were out on VHS in August 1996 and would continue though to 1998. A while back members of Evageeks discovered a tape containing, after some clarification/investigation from myself, a bootleg fansub of episode 1 using the JP VHS version (Feb 96) as its source. In short, a peculiar quirk of Japan's release schedule and ADV's eagerness resulted in ADV getting On-Air versions of Eva TV to home release sooner than Japan, but the NPC/Director's Cut versions and the movies wouldn't make it to the west until 2002-2004. To be blunt, I don't think there was much to be a fan of outside of an incomplete series, some niche mail-order bootlegging, and maybe some peer-to-peer internet piracy like Kazaa.

[]: Re: How exactly was Eva released in the USA?             Quote Post by BritKnee » Sat May 21, 2022 5:15 am Szmitten wrote: To be blunt, I don't think there was much to be a fan of outside of an incomplete series, some niche mail-order bootlegging, and maybe some peer-to-peer internet piracy like Kazaa.

[]: Re: How exactly was Eva released in the USA?             Quote Post by Szmitten » Sat May 21, 2022 7:55 am The Red Cross Book was a pamphlet released with EoE that served as a glossary and was a bible for early 00s Eva fandom, yes.

[]: Re: How exactly was Eva released in the USA?             Quote Post by BritKnee » Sat May 21, 2022 9:25 am Szmitten wrote: The Red Cross Book was a pamphlet released with EoE that served as a glossary and was a bible for early 00s Eva fandom, yes.

[]: Re: How exactly was Eva released in the USA?             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Tue May 24, 2022 6:10 am No, that sort of info would come in later proper guides. Usually you see an image from Evangelion Chronicle. It's on the wiki and e-hentai. I'm not that old of a fan but I can generally say it was all funny and messy those days. Read this article: https://www.kotaku.com.au/2012/10/anime ... d-spoiled/
